D. Teaching Compensatory Techniques to Improve the Level of Independence in 
the Activities of Daily Living. 

EXAMPLE: Teaching a patient who has lost use of an arm how to pare potatoes and 
chop vegetables with one hand. 

EXAMPLE: Teaching a stroke patient new techniques to enable them to perform 
feeding, dressing, and other activities of daily living as independently as possible.
